element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial level with basic movement and firing mechanics
Player learns to toggle between air and ground levels; ground immunity to airborne attacks but vulnerable to ground units
First weapon power-up introduces damage increase and spread
Teaches spread pattern and collision-reset mechanic
Defensive weapon teaches close-range protection strategy
Expands attack surface area with left/right/center spread
Teaches rear-guard strategy for enemies approaching from behind
Requires timing; projectile must explode on target for damage
Famicom exclusive; introduces damage mitigation alongside offense
Famicom exclusive; piercing enables boss phase strategy
Famicom exclusive; sustained damage against bosses
Mandatory pattern-learning confrontations; weapon choice determines strategy
Enables weapon swapping via pause menu; one-use consumable
Risk/reward trade (boss skip vs. point penalty)
Famicom exclusive; secret weapon with shield + dyna-like behavior
Famicom exclusive; secret weapon with omnidirectional coverage
Famicom exclusive; secret weapon; maximum coverage but short range

typical_relief_triggers: Descending to ground level (air-attack immunity window)
Collecting Voltage item for weapon swap safety
typical_tension_triggers: Forced auto-ascent from ground level after timer expires
Ground unit attacks while on ground level
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade shmup rhythm with escalating density; early levels establish weapon switching, mid-game intensifies enemy patterns and introduces defensive play, late-game combines complex enemy waves with boss pattern memorization

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden weapons beneath obstacles and scattered throughout levels; ground/air level toggle provides spatial depth and alternate routes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Obstacles on ground level conceal Sterling-Silver, Aurora-Harrier, and Oct-Blaster weapons; Comet warp items hidden similarly

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial pacing with single-shot base weapon and simple enemy patterns; ground descent mechanic introduced without pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon collection becomes mandatory for progression; enemy density increases; defensive wings (Van, Side) required for survival; mid-range bosses introduce pattern learning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-wave enemy formations; boss encounters demand specific weapon strategies; hidden secret weapons essential for optimal runs; Jump, Hammer, Dyna, Fire wings require precise timing and positioning
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (introduces mandatory pattern learning)
Mid-game boss sequences (enemies become mobile and predictive)
Late-game secret weapon bosses (require piercing/sustained damage strategies)
Final level bosses (culmination of all mechanics and pattern knowledge)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels introduce each standard wing type sequentially with moderate enemy density to allow player experimentation and mastery before boss
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Levels 1-6 (Wingless → Cannon → Wide → Multi → Van → Side)

notable_level_patterns.description: Every level terminates with a Gobunasu boss fight; boss design telegraphs which weapon type is effective for that encounter
Continuous vertical auto-scroll punctuated by horizontal reposition opportunities; ground level provides tactical pause points
Specific mid-late game levels hide Sterling-Silver, Aurora-Harrier, Oct-Blaster under obstacles; encourages exploration and revisit
